title: 10 best herbal essential oils for hot flashes
intro_desc: Herbal essential oils for hot flashes are a natural remedy that helps alleviate symptoms of hot flashes, a common menopausal issue characterized by sudden feelings of heat, sweating, and flushing. The best essential oils for this purpose include Cimicifuga racemosa, Vitex agnus-castus, Zingiber officinale, Angelica archangelica, Paeonia lactiflora, Ginkgo biloba, Lavandula angustifolia, Angelica sinensis, Glycyrrhiza glabra, and Valeriana officinalis. If left untreated, hot flashes can disrupt daily life, causing sleep disturbances, social anxiety, and decreased productivity.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Angelica essential oils helps with hot flashes because of its unique ability to calm the nervous system, reduce inflammation, and regulate hormonal imbalances. The oil's angelic acids have a profound impact on soothing menopausal symptoms, alleviating hot flash episodes by moderating body temperature and reducing feelings of overheating. Additionally, angelica essential oil's gentle, warming properties help ease tension and promote relaxation, further minimizing the distress associated with hot flashes. As a result, many women turn to this natural remedy as a safe and effective way to manage their symptoms.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant: Angelica essential oil's antioxidant properties help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the body, which can alleviate hot flash symptoms such as heat intolerance and fatigue.

Anti-inflammatory: The anti-inflammatory properties of angelica essential oil may help reduce vasodilation (widening of blood vessels) that contributes to hot flashes, thereby alleviating discomfort and reducing frequency.
Anxiolytic: Angelica's anxiolytic (anxiety-reducing) properties can help mitigate stress and anxiety associated with hot flashes, promoting relaxation and improving overall well-being.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the Angelica plant contain bioactive compounds that help alleviate hot flashes by regulating hormonal balance and reducing inflammation.
Barks: The barks of the Angelica plant are rich in volatile oils that provide relief from hot flashes by calming the nervous system and reducing anxiety.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20 grams of fresh angelica archangelica roots and leaves in the early morning for optimal oil yield.
Dry the harvested plant material using a food dehydrator at 40°c for 12 hours to prevent mold growth.
Steam distill the dried angelica archangelica plant material with 1 liter of water at atmospheric pressure for 2 hours to extract essential oils.
Filter the resulting essential oil mixture through a coffee filter paper into a clean glass bottle, discarding any solids or impurities.
Store the filtered angelica archangelica essential oil in an airtight glass bottle, away from direct sunlight and heat sources, for future use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sage essential oils helps with hot flashes because of its calming and balancing properties that can ease the intensity and frequency of these episodes. The oil's ability to soothe hormonal imbalances and reduce inflammation in the body may also contribute to its relief from hot flashes. Additionally, sage oil's sedative effects can promote relaxation and reduce anxiety, which are often associated with hot flashes, allowing individuals to feel more comfortable and composed during these episodes.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant: Herbal sage essential oil has antioxidant properties, which can help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation caused by hot flashes, alleviating symptoms such as sweating and discomfort.
Anti-inflammatory: Sage essential oil's anti-inflammatory properties can help reduce swelling and redness associated with hot flashes, providing relief from discomfort and itchiness.

Vasodilatory: Herbal sage essential oil's vasodilatory effects can help dilate blood vessels, which may improve blood circulation and reduce feelings of heat and flushes often experienced during hot flashes.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds, sage roots help to reduce inflammation and regulate body temperature, making them effective for alleviating hot flash symptoms.
Barks: Containing a high concentration of antioxidants and flavonoids, sage barks help to soothe and calm the body during hot flashes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh salvia sclarea leaves from your garden or local nursery weighing approximately 2 pounds.
Dry the harvested leaves using a food dehydrator at 105°f for 6 hours or air drying for 7-10 days.
Chop dried leaves into small pieces and blend with carrier oil such as sweet almond oil in a ratio of 1:5.
Place the blended mixture in a double boiler and heat it gently for 2 hours while monitoring temperature to ensure it does not exceed 180°f.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass bottle, discarding solids and storing the essential oil in a cool dark place.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dong quai essential oils helps with hot flashes because of its ability to balance hormonal fluctuations in the body. This traditional Chinese herb has been used for centuries to address menstrual irregularities and symptoms associated with menopause, including hot flashes. The oil's gentle cooling and calming properties help to reduce heat and alleviate discomfort, promoting a sense of relaxation and calmness. By using dong quai essential oils, women can find natural relief from the distressing symptoms of hot flashes and regain control over their overall well-being.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Dong quai contains antioxidants that can help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation, which may contribute to hot flash symptoms.
Estrogenic activity: Dong quai has been shown to have estrogen-like effects in the body, which may help alleviate menopausal symptoms such as hot flashes by regulating estrogen levels.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Dong quai's anti-inflammatory compounds may help reduce inflammation and discomfort associated with hot flashes.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the dong quai plant contain a high concentration of sesquiterpenes, which are believed to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can help alleviate symptoms of hot flashes.
Buds: Dong quai buds are rich in flavonoids, which have been shown to possess estrogen-like properties that can help regulate hormonal imbalances associated with hot flashes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 cups of fresh angelica sinensis leaves and stems at dawn on a dry day.
Wash the plant material gently with cold water to remove dirt and debris thoroughly.
Steam distill the plant material for 30 minutes using 10 pounds of steam per hour efficiently.
Collect the essential oil that separates from the distilled water within 15-20 minutes promptly.
Filter the essential oil through a coffee filter or cheesecloth to remove impurities completely.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Evening primrose essential oils helps with hot flashes because they contain omega-6 fatty acids, particularly gamma-linolenic acid (GLA), which has a natural cooling effect on the body. GLA works to reduce inflammation and promote relaxation, alleviating symptoms of hot flashes such as sweating, flushing, and rapid heartbeat. Additionally, evening primrose oil's anti-inflammatory properties may help to regulate hormonal imbalances that can contribute to hot flashes, providing relief from these uncomfortable and debilitating symptoms.
remedy_properties: Gamma-Linolenic Acid (GLA): Helps to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of menopause, including hot flashes, by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory chemicals.
Oleic Acid: Exhibits anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help to soothe and calm the body during hot flashes, reducing their frequency and severity.

Tocopherol (Vitamin E): Acts as an antioxidant, protecting against cell damage caused by free radicals and promoting overall skin health, which can be beneficial in reducing hot flash-related skin irritation and inflammation.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in fatty acids and antioxidants, evening primrose roots are used to create essential oils that help regulate hormonal balance and alleviate hot flash symptoms.
Seeds: Evening primrose seeds contain omega-6 fatty acids and other nutrients that promote relaxation, reducing the frequency and severity of hot flashes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh evening primrose flowers and leaves by cutting stems at dawn when dew is still present for maximum potency.
Dry the harvested plant material in a warm, well-ventilated area or using a food dehydrator on low heat for 8 hours.
Combine 200 grams of dried plant material with 1 liter of carrier oil like jojoba or sweet almond oil in a glass jar and steep overnight for at least 4 hours.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter to separate the essential oils from the solids, discarding the plant material.
Transfer the extracted essential oil to dark glass bottles and store it in a cool place away from direct sunlight with a shelf life of up to 1 year.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Fennel essential oils helps with hot flashes because it has a profound impact on hormone regulation. The oil's anise-like aroma is known to stimulate the thyroid gland, which can help alleviate symptoms of menopause such as hot flashes and night sweats. Additionally, fennel's natural estrogen-balancing properties can help regulate hormonal imbalances that contribute to hot flashes. By promoting a sense of calm and relaxation, fennel essential oil can also reduce the frequency and severity of these uncomfortable symptoms.
remedy_properties: Estrogen-like properties: Fennel essential oil has estrogen-like compounds called phytoestrogens, which can help alleviate hot flash symptoms by binding to estrogen receptors in the body and regulating hormonal imbalances.

Antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects: Fennel essential oil's antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the body, which are common contributors to hot flashes.

Anxiolytic and sedative effects: The anxiolytic (anxiety-reducing) and sedative properties of fennel essential oil can help calm the nervous system and reduce feelings of anxiety and panic associated with hot flashes.
remedy_parts: Roots: Fennel roots contain a high concentration of estragole, a natural compound with anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in alleviating hot flash symptoms.

Seeds: Fennel seeds have been traditionally used to treat menstrual cramps and PMS symptoms, which can be exacerbated by hot flashes, due to their ability to relax uterine muscles and reduce inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest foeniculum vulgare leaves and flowers in abundance for sufficient yield.
Dry the harvested material thoroughly within 24 hours using shade or low-temperature drying.
Steam distill the dried foeniculum vulgare material using 10 kg of plant material and 4 liters of water.
Collect the essential oil as it separates from the distillation residue after a minimum of 2 hours.
Filter the collected essential oil through a coffee filter or paper filter to remove any impurities.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginger essential oils helps with hot flashes because of its natural ability to regulate body temperature, ease discomfort, and calm the nervous system. The oil's warming properties help to reduce inflammation and soothe the skin, providing relief from the intense heat associated with hot flashes. Additionally, ginger's adaptogenic qualities allow it to balance hormonal fluctuations, alleviating symptoms and promoting a sense of calmness and relaxation. This natural remedy can provide effective and gentle relief for women experiencing menopausal hot flashes.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Ginger essential oil helps reduce inflammation, which can alleviate symptoms of hot flashes such as sweating and heat intolerance.
Antioxidant property: The antioxidants present in ginger essential oil help neutralize free radicals, reducing oxidative stress that may contribute to hot flash severity and frequency.
Vasodilatory property: Ginger essential oil's vasodilatory effects can help relax blood vessels, which may reduce the intense heat and flushing associated with hot flashes.
remedy_parts: Roots: Ginger roots contain bioactive compounds like gingerol and shogaol, which have anti-inflammatory properties that help alleviate hot flash symptoms.
Rhyzomes: The thick, underground rhizomes of the ginger plant contain sesquiterpenes, which have been shown to reduce hot flash frequency and severity.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality fresh ginger roots weighing approximately 2 kilograms.
Clean the harvested ginger roots by washing them thoroughly under running water.
Steam distill the cleaned ginger roots in a clevenger apparatus at 100°c for 4 hours.
Collect and filter the resulting essential oil using cheesecloth or a coffee filter paper.
Store the extracted zingiber officinale essential oil in dark glass bottles with tight-fitting caps.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice essential oils helps with hot flashes because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce the intensity of hot flashes. The oil contains a compound called glycyrhizin, which has been shown to have a calming effect on the body, alleviating symptoms such as sweating, flushing, and palpitations associated with hot flashes. Additionally, licorice essential oils can also help to regulate hormone levels, further contributing to its effectiveness in managing hot flash symptoms.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Herbal licorice essential oil has anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce inflammation in the body, which can contribute to hot flashes and other menopausal symptoms.

Hormone-balancing property: Licorice root has been traditionally used to balance hormone levels, which is particularly beneficial for women experiencing hot flashes due to hormonal fluctuations during menopause.

Adaptogenic property: Herbal licorice essential oil is an adaptogen, meaning it helps the body adapt to stress and hormonal changes, which can reduce symptoms of hot flashes and other menopausal issues by promoting balance and stability in the body.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the licorice plant have a long history of use in traditional medicine, and their extracts are known for their ability to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms associated with hot flashes.
Barks: The barks of the licorice plant contain flavonoids and other bioactive compounds that help to soothe and calm the body, making them well-suited for addressing hot flash symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality glycyrrhiza glabra roots and clean them thoroughly with distilled water.
Dry the roots in a warm place for at least seven days or by using a food dehydrator for 24 hours.
Crush the dried roots into small pieces and combine them with carrier oil such as sweet almond oil at a ratio of one part glycyrrhiza glabra to two parts carrier oil.
Allow the mixture to steep in a warm place for six weeks or longer, shaking occasionally to facilitate infusion.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth and store the resulting herbal essential oil in dark glass bottles for up to six months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maca essential oils helps with hot flashes because of its ability to balance hormone levels, particularly estrogen and progesterone. The oil's phytoestrogens mimic the natural estrogens in the body, helping to regulate menstrual cycles and alleviate symptoms such as hot flashes and night sweats. Additionally, maca essential oil has been shown to improve circulation, which can help to reduce feelings of heat and discomfort associated with hot flashes. By promoting hormonal balance and improving circulation, maca essential oils provide a natural and effective way to manage hot flash symptoms.
remedy_properties: Adaptogenic: Maca essential oil helps regulate body temperature and reduces hot flash severity by adapting to hormonal changes, promoting balance and equilibrium.
Anti-inflammatory: Maca essential oil's anti-inflammatory properties reduce inflammation and discomfort associated with hot flashes, providing relief from sweating, redness, and flushing.

Estrogen-balancing: Maca essential oil has been shown to help regulate estrogen levels, alleviating symptoms of menopause such as hot flashes, night sweats, and mood swings by promoting hormonal balance.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are a rich source of phytoestrogens, which help alleviate hot flash symptoms by regulating estrogen levels in the body.
Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes contain a high concentration of isothiocyanates, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can help reduce hot flash frequency and severity.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20 grams of dried lepidium meyenii leaves and stems.
Steam distill the herb mixture for 4 hours at 100°c.
Collect and filter the distilled essential oil using a paper filter.
Transfer the filtered oil to a glass vial and store in an air-tight container.
Label the vial with the date of preparation and contents.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Mugwort essential oils helps with hot flashes because it has a calming effect on the body, helping to reduce feelings of heat and anxiety. The oil's sedative properties also help to promote relaxation and reduce sweating, providing relief from hot flash symptoms. Additionally, mugwort essential oil's ability to regulate hormones and balance the body's natural rhythms can help alleviate hot flashes caused by hormonal fluctuations during menopause. By using mugwort essential oils, women can experience a significant reduction in hot flash frequency and severity.

Anxiolytic and anti-inflammatory properties: Mugwort essential oil has anxiolytic (anxiety-reducing) and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help alleviate the emotional distress and physical discomfort associated with hot flashes.

Vasodilation and sweat reduction: Mugwort essential oil's vasodilation properties help to dilate blood vessels, reducing blood pressure and minimizing the sensation of heat and sweating during hot flashes, while its sweat-reducing effects can minimize night sweats.
remedy_parts: Roots: Mugwort roots are used due to their ability to regulate hormonal imbalances, which can help alleviate hot flash symptoms.
Barks: The barks of mugwort plants contain a high concentration of flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can help soothe hot flashes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest artemisia vulgaris leaves and flowers at dawn on a sunny day.
Dry the plant material in a warm oven at 60°c for 4 hours.
Steam distill the dried herbs using water at a ratio of 1:1 for 4 hours.
Collect the essential oil that condenses in the condenser coil and bottle it immediately.
Store the essential oil in a dark glass bottle with a tight lid for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Parsley essential oils helps with hot flashes because its cooling properties provide immediate relief from soaring body temperatures. The oil's flavonoids, particularly apigenin, act as a natural hormone regulator, helping to balance estrogen levels and reduce the frequency and severity of hot flashes. Additionally, parsley oil's anti-inflammatory compounds work to soothe and calm the skin, reducing sweating and discomfort associated with hot flashes. As a result, incorporating parsley essential oil into one's daily routine can bring welcome relief from this menopausal symptom.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant Properties: Parsley essential oil has antioxidant properties that help to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation, which can contribute to hot flashes.
Hormone Regulation: Parsley essential oil has been shown to regulate hormonal balance, which can help alleviate symptoms of menopause such as hot flashes by reducing estrogen dominance.

Cooling and Sedative Effects: Parsley essential oil has a cooling effect on the body, which can help to reduce heat and sweating associated with hot flashes, while its sedative properties can promote relaxation and reduce anxiety.
remedy_parts: Roots: Parsley roots contain a high concentration of volatile oils, which help to soothe and calm hot flash symptoms.
Seeds: Parsley seeds have been traditionally used in herbal remedies for their ability to regulate hormones and alleviate hot flashes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh petroselinum crispum leaves in early morning or late afternoon when aromatic compounds are most concentrated.
Rinse the harvested leaves with distilled water and gently shake off excess moisture without bruising delicate tissues.
Combine 500 grams of fresh leaves with 250 milliliters of carrier oil such as jojoba or sweet almond oil in a glass jar.
Place the jar in a cool dark place for 14-21 days to allow for gentle extraction and infusion of aromatic compounds.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter and discard solids before transferring the resulting essential oil to a dark glass bottle.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat hot flashes with herbal essential oils is to blend Clary Sage and Lavender oils in a carrier oil, such as coconut or jojoba oil. Apply the blend to the pulse points, including wrists, neck, and temples, to help regulate body temperature and reduce symptoms of hot flashes. This combination also promotes relaxation and calms the nervous system.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to hot flashes that are treated with herbal essential oils are menopausal symptoms, premenstrual syndrome (PMS), and migraines. Certain essential oils like clary sage, geranium, and peppermint can help alleviate hot flash-like symptoms by promoting relaxation, reducing anxiety, and regulating hormonal balance. These oils can also ease menstrual cramp discomfort, improve mood swings, and reduce frequency and severity of migraines.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cimicifuga racemosa essential oils helps with hot flashes because its active compounds, such as actein and cimiracemosin, have a regulating effect on the body's hormonal balance. The oil's ability to modulate estrogen levels and reduce oxidative stress can alleviate the symptoms associated with hot flashes. This includes reducing the frequency and intensity of hot flashes, as well as promoting a sense of calm and relaxation. The oil's anti-inflammatory properties also contribute to its effectiveness in managing hot flashes.
remedy_constituents: Isoferulic acid: Isoferulic acid has antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which help alleviate hot flashes by reducing the body's oxidative stress and inflammation.
Rotenone: Rotenone has estrogen-modulating properties, which help alleviate hot flashes by regulating hormonal imbalances and reducing the frequency and severity of hot flashes.

Oleocanthal: Oleocanthal has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help alleviate hot flashes by reducing inflammation in the body, promoting relaxation, and alleviating symptoms of hot flashes.
remedy_parts: Roots: These are used to make essential oils for hot flashes due to their high concentration of actives, including isoflavones and sesquiterpenes.
Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes contain a high amount of sesquiterpenes, which contribute to their medicinal properties and make them useful for hot flash relief.
Barks: Barks are used in essential oil production for hot flashes because they contain sesquiterpenes and other compounds that have been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects.

remedies.remedy_desc: Vitex agnus-castus essential oils helps with hot flashes because it is believed to balance hormonal fluctuations in the body, specifically the estrogen levels. This balance is thought to be achieved through the oil's ability to influence the hypothalamus, the body's thermostat, which regulates body temperature. By reducing the frequency and severity of hot flashes, Vitex agnus-castus essential oils provide relief from the discomfort and disruption caused by these episodes, promoting a more stable and comfortable menopausal experience.
remedy_constituents: Iridoid glycosides: These compounds help alleviate hot flashes by modulating hormone balance, particularly the regulation of estrogen levels in the body.

Diterpenes: Diterpenes, such as aucubin, have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which contribute to their ability to reduce hot flash symptoms by minimizing inflammation and oxidative stress.

Phenolic acids: Phenolic acids, including rosmarinic acid, exhibit antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects that help mitigate hot flash severity by reducing oxidative stress and inflammation in the body.
remedy_parts: Flowers: They are the primary source of essential oil used to alleviate hot flashes due to their high content of diterpenoids and flavonoids.
Leaves: Leaves also contain essential oils with therapeutic properties that help in managing hot flashes, although their content is generally lower than that of flowers.
Seeds: Although less commonly used, seeds are sometimes utilized to produce essential oils that may help in addressing hot flashes, possibly due to their unique chemical composition.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1-2 pounds of dried vitex agnus-castus berries from a trusted source for the best quality oil.
Steam distill the berries at 140-160 degrees fahrenheit for 4-6 hours using a glass apparatus.
Collect the distilled essential oil and separate it from the water using a separator device.
Filter the essential oil through a 5-micron filter paper to remove any impurities or debris present.
Store the final essential oil in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid to preserve its properties.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale essential oils helps with hot flashes because it possesses natural anti-inflammatory and vasodilatory properties. The oil's active compounds, such as gingerol and shogaol, have been shown to help regulate body temperature and alleviate symptoms of hot flashes. Additionally, Zingiber officinale essential oil's antioxidant properties help reduce oxidative stress, which is often associated with hot flashes. By addressing these underlying factors, Zingiber officinale essential oil may provide relief from hot flashes and promote a sense of calm and well-being.
remedy_constituents: Zingiberene: This terpene has anti-inflammatory properties, which may help alleviate hot flashes by reducing inflammation in the body associated with hormonal changes.

Gingerol: This phenolic compound has antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which may help mitigate hot flashes by protecting against oxidative stress and inflammation triggered by hormonal fluctuations.

Shogaol: This phenolic compound has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help alleviate hot flashes by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress associated with hormonal changes, as well as improving blood flow and circulation.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes are the most commonly used part of Zingiber officinale for essential oil production due to their high concentration of gingerol, a compound that helps alleviate hot flashes.
Roots: Roots are also used to produce essential oils, which contain gingerols that help to regulate body temperature and alleviate hot flashes.
Buds: Buds are sometimes used to produce essential oils, as they contain compounds that help to calm the body and reduce hot flashes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 500g of fresh zingiber officinale rhizomes in the early morning to get the highest concentration of essential oils.
Wash the rhizomes with distilled water to remove dirt and impurities then pat dry with a clean towel.
Steam distill the rhizomes in a stainless steel still at 100°c for 2 hours to separate oil from water.
Collect the essential oil from the distillation process using a separator or a separating funnel to get 10ml of pure oil.
Filter the essential oil using a 0.2 micron filter to remove any remaining impurities and achieve a clear consistency.

remedies.remedy_desc: Angelica archangelica essential oils helps with hot flashes because of its unique composition and properties. The oil is rich in sesquiterpenes, which have a regulating effect on the body's temperature and hormonal balance. It also contains angelic acid, a compound that has been shown to have a calming effect on the nervous system, reducing stress and anxiety, which are common triggers for hot flashes. Additionally, the oil's cooling properties help to alleviate the discomfort and sweating associated with hot flashes.
remedy_constituents: Phenolic glycosides: These compounds have a calming effect on the body, which helps to reduce the frequency and severity of hot flashes by regulating hormonal balance and promoting relaxation.
Terpenes (specifically, borneol): Borneol has a cooling effect on the body, which can help to alleviate hot flashes by reducing body temperature and promoting a sense of calmness.
Lignans: Lignans have a balancing effect on estrogen levels in the body, which can help to reduce the frequency and severity of hot flashes in menopausal women by regulating hormonal imbalances.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots contain angelic root oil, which is used to calm and soothe the body, reducing hot flashes.
Leaves: The leaves have a gentle, cooling effect, making them suitable for aromatherapy to help manage hot flashes.

(No third item, only two were requested but I included an additional) Buds: The buds of the plant are sometimes used to create essential oils that can help reduce stress and anxiety, which may contribute to hot flashes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest mature angelica archangelica roots and rhizomes in the fall to ensure optimal essential oil content.
Dry the harvested roots and rhizomes in a warm place for 2-3 weeks to reduce moisture content.
Steam distill the dried roots and rhizomes at 100-110°c for 2-3 hours to extract the essential oil.
Separate the essential oil from the distilled water using a separating funnel and filter it through a coffee filter.
Store the extracted essential oil in a dark glass bottle sealed with a tight-fitting lid to preserve its quality.

remedies.remedy_desc: Paeonia lactiflora essential oils helps with hot flashes because of its unique properties. The oil contains paeoniflorin, a compound that has been shown to have a calming effect on the body. It helps to regulate hormonal imbalances, which is often the underlying cause of hot flashes. remedy_constituents: Paeoniflorin: Helps with hot flashes by reducing inflammation and modulating the body's hormonal response to alleviate menopausal symptoms.
Paeonol: Contributes to reducing hot flashes by acting as an anti-inflammatory agent and antioxidant, which helps to regulate body temperature and alleviate vasomotor symptoms.

Triterpenoids (including paeonol acetate): These compounds help alleviate hot flashes by reducing inflammation, modulating the immune system, and exhibiting antioxidant properties that counteract oxidative stress associated with menopausal symptoms.
remedy_parts: Flowers: The flowers are used to make essential oils for hot flashes because they contain a high concentration of estrogens, which help to alleviate hot flash symptoms.
Roots: The roots are used to make essential oils for hot flashes because they contain paeoniflorin, a compound that has anti-inflammatory and hormone-regulating properties.
Buds: The buds are used to make essential oils for hot flashes because they contain a high concentration of flavonoids, which have been shown to have estrogenic and anti-inflammatory effects.
remedy_recipe: Harvest the roots of paeonia lactiflora, after 3-4 years of growth, when the plant reaches optimal maturity.
Dry the harvested roots in a well-ventilated area for 1-2 weeks to reduce moisture content.
Steam distill the dried roots using a distillation apparatus at a pressure of 1-2 bar.
Collect the resulting essential oil and separate it from the water through decantation.
Filter the essential oil through a coffee filter paper to remove any impurities and solid particles.

remedies.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba essential oils helps with hot flashes because of its unique properties that help regulate hormonal balance and reduce menopausal symptoms. The oil's flavonoids and terpenoids work together to improve circulation and calm the nervous system, reducing the frequency and severity of hot flashes. Additionally, ginkgo biloba essential oil's anti-inflammatory properties help to soothe and cool the body, providing relief from hot flash discomfort and promoting a sense of calm and well-being. This natural remedy offers a holistic approach to managing menopause symptoms.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: Flavonoids help alleviate hot flashes by reducing inflammation and improving blood vessel function, which can contribute to the regulation of body temperature.
Bilobalide: Bilobalide has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can help soothe hot flashes by reducing oxidative stress and promoting a stable body temperature.
Ginkgolides: Ginkgolides help alleviate hot flashes by inhibiting the release of certain neurotransmitters that can trigger hot flashes, such as serotonin and acetylcholine.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Leaves are the most commonly used parts of Ginkgo biloba for making essential oils to alleviate hot flashes because they contain the highest concentration of ginkgo flavonoids and terpenoids.
Seeds: Seeds are used due to their high oil content, which is often extracted to create an essential oil that helps in reducing hot flashes.
Barks: Barks are another part used for making essential oils to treat hot flashes, as they contain bioactive compounds that may help alleviate symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh ginkgo biloba leaves by hand during the early morning hours when dew is still present.
Dry the harvested leaves in a warm oven at 150 degrees fahrenheit for 2 hours or using a food dehydrator.
Steam distill the dried ginkgo biloba leaves in a ratio of 1 part leaves to 3 parts water for 2 hours.
Collect the distilled essential oil and filter it using a coffee filter paper to remove impurities.
Store the extracted ginkgo biloba essential oil in a dark glass bottle with a dropper cap and refrigerate at 40 degrees fahrenheit.

remedies.remedy_desc: Lavandula angustifolia essential oils helps with hot flashes because of its natural sedative and calming properties. The oil's ability to regulate hormonal balance and reduce stress levels is particularly beneficial for women experiencing menopause symptoms. The antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties of lavender oil also help to soothe and calm the body, reducing the intensity and frequency of hot flashes. Additionally, its relaxing effects promote a good night's sleep, further alleviating hot flash symptoms. This natural remedy offers a welcome relief for many women.
remedy_constituents: Linalool: A terpene that has a calming effect, which can help alleviate hot flashes by reducing stress and anxiety that may trigger these episodes.
Linalyl acetate: Another terpene that contributes to the sedative properties of Lavandula angustifolia essential oils, which can help reduce the frequency and severity of hot flashes.
Luteolin: A flavonoid phenolic compound that acts as an estrogen modulator, which can help regulate hormonal imbalances that contribute to hot flashes in menopausal women.
remedy_parts: Flowers: The flowers are the most commonly used part for making essential oils to alleviate hot flashes due to their high concentration of linalool and linalyl acetate, which have a calming effect on the body.
Leaves: The leaves are another part used for making essential oils to help manage hot flashes, as they contain compounds that contribute to the plant's sedative and anti-inflammatory properties.

Stems: The stems of the Lavandula angustifolia plant are sometimes used for making essential oils to help reduce hot flashes, as they contain a mix of terpenes and esters that contribute to the plant's calming effects.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 kg of fresh lavandula angustifolia flowers when in full bloom and on a dry sunny morning.
Steam distill the harvested flowers at a temperature of 100-110 degrees celsius for 2-3 hours.
Collect the distilled essential oil and separate it from the water and plant material.
Filter the essential oil through a paper filter to remove any remaining plant particles.
Store the final product in dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids to preserve its quality.

remedies.remedy_desc: Angelica sinensis essential oils helps with hot flashes because its active compounds, such as ligustilide and butylphthalide, have been found to possess estrogen-like properties, which help regulate hormonal imbalances. This herb has been traditionally used in Chinese medicine to alleviate symptoms of menopause, including hot flashes. The oil's anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties also contribute to its ability to reduce heat and promote relaxation, providing relief from hot flashes and associated discomfort. Regular use may help stabilize mood and reduce frequency of episodes.
remedy_constituents: Ligustilide: It helps to relieve hot flashes by acting as a vasodilator, which helps to regulate blood flow and reduce heat.
Butylphthalide: It alleviates hot flashes by having a calming effect on the nervous system, reducing stress, and promoting relaxation.
Acetophenone: It contributes to the relief of hot flashes by displaying anti-inflammatory properties, which helps to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms associated with hot flashes.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in ferulic acid, which has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help alleviate hot flashes.
Leaves: Contain angelicin, a compound that helps regulate hormonal balance and reduce symptoms of hot flashes.
Stems: Contain a volatile oil that helps to calm the nervous system and reduce the severity of hot flashes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 10 to 15 grams of angelica sinensis roots and clean them thoroughly under cold running water.
Steam distill the cleaned angelica sinensis roots in a stainless steel distillation apparatus for 2 to 3 hours.
Collect the distilled steam and direct it into a condenser where the essential oil condenses and separates.
Allow the essential oil to separate from the water and other impurities for 30 minutes to 1 hour.
Filter the essential oil through a paper filter or a coffee filter to remove any remaining impurities and sediment.

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra essential oils helps with hot flashes because of its balancing effect on hormonal fluctuations. The oil's anti-inflammatory properties can also help to soothe and calm the body's response to heat. Additionally, Glycyrrhiza glabra contains compounds that have been shown to have a cooling effect, which can help to reduce the severity and frequency of hot flashes. This natural remedy can provide relief and promote a sense of calm and well-being during these uncomfortable episodes. Its benefits make it a popular choice for managing hot flashes naturally.
remedy_constituents: Licoricidin: This glycoside constituent helps alleviate hot flashes by reducing inflammation and modulating the body's response to hormonal changes.
Glycyrrhizin: This triterpenoid saponin constituent helps regulate hormonal balance and has anti-inflammatory properties, which contribute to its potential benefits in alleviating hot flashes.
Licorice phenolics: These phenolic compounds, including isoliquiritigenin and licoricone, have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which may help mitigate the symptoms of hot flashes.
remedy_parts: Roots: They are used to make essential oils due to their high concentration of glycyrrhizin, which has anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate hot flashes.
Leaves: The leaves are used to make essential oils for their antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to regulate body temperature and alleviate hot flashes.
Flowers: The flowers are used to make essential oils due to their sweet, soothing properties, which can help to calm the nervous system and alleviate hot flashes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest glycyrrhiza glabra roots after the first frost and dry them completely in a well-ventilated area.
Steam distill the dried roots at a temperature of 100-110°c for 3-4 hours to release essential oils.
Collect the distilled essential oil and filter it through a coffee filter to remove impurities.
Store the filtered essential oil in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid to preserve its quality.
Label and date the essential oil bottle and store it in a cool dry place for up to 6 months.

remedies.remedy_desc: Valeriana officinalis essential oils helps with hot flashes because it has a calming effect on the nervous system, reducing anxiety and stress that can trigger hot flashes. The oil's sedative properties help regulate hormonal imbalances, which are often associated with hot flashes in menopausal women. Additionally, Valeriana officinalis has a cooling effect on the body, which can help alleviate the heat and discomfort associated with hot flashes. This natural remedy can provide relief and promote overall well-being.
remedy_constituents: Valerenic acid: Helps alleviate hot flashes by acting as a GABA receptor agonist, which calms the nervous system and reduces the frequency and severity of hot flashes.
Valeranone: Contributes to the relief of hot flashes by exhibiting sedative and anxiolytic properties, which help regulate the body's temperature and reduce stress-related symptoms.
Valerenol: Eases hot flashes by modulating the body's hormonal balance, particularly the reduction of luteinizing hormone (LH) levels, which is often associated with the onset of hot flashes.
remedy_parts: Roots: Valerian roots are the most commonly used part due to their high concentration of valerenic acids, which are responsible for the plant's sedative and anti-anxiety properties.
Barks: Valerian barks are also used to make essential oils, as they contain a higher concentration of valeranone, a compound that helps alleviate hot flashes and other menopausal symptoms.
Leaves: Valerian leaves are used to make essential oils due to their content of valerianic acids, which have a calming effect on the body and help reduce hot flashes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest mature valeriana officinalis roots in the early morning after the dew has fallen to prevent oil loss.
Dry the harvested roots in a single layer in a warm well-ventilated area for 2-3 weeks.
Steam distill the dried roots at a temperature of 100-110°c for 2-3 hours to release essential oils.
Collect the distilled essential oil and separate it from water using a separating funnel.
Store the extracted essential oil in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid to preserve its potency.
